query_normalized: the impact of parasocial relationships with ai companions on loneliness psychology
rationale: The paper empirically examines how interaction with AI companions (a form of parasocial relationship) affects users’ loneliness, directly measuring the same dependent variable (loneliness) and a comparable independent variable (AI companion use) as the user’s question (category (b)). It therefore provides relevant evidence for the impact of parasocial AI relationships on loneliness psychology.
